title: narcissus nevadensis subsp. longispathus
intro: Narcissus nevadensis subsp. longispathus, also known as the long-petaled Nevada daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine and lycorine. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the potential to reduce anxiety and improve cognitive function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of Narcissus nevadensis subsp. longispathus are often dried and used as a tincture or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
